questions.question: Which of the following describes the operational restriction status and reason for Salar de Uyuni Sector 4 (Bolivia)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No PAMC operations authorized due to a pending bilateral agreement.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Limited access with prior coordination required, due to an ongoing classified PAMC investigation since 2023.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Complete exclusion zone because of psychological and CBMP recommendations; the asset has not entered since 2020.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Active operational zone requiring quarterly rotation of assets.

questions.explanation: Section 8.6 states that Salar de Uyuni Sector 4 (Bolivia) is restricted due to a 'Strategic; ongoing classified PAMC investigation since 2023' and its status is 'Limited access, prior coordination required'.

questions.question: Which of the following options is NOT part of the standardized safehouse provisioning standard maintained at each of the five operational safehouses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 30-day rations for 4 personnel

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yatiri-2 backup comms unit

choices.label: C
choices.text: 4 Calexin-7 spare blister packs

choices.label: D
choices.text: 2,000 USD-equivalent local currency in mixed denominations

questions.explanation: Section 8.3 lists '2 Calexin-7 spare blister packs' as the standard safehouse provisioning requirement, not 4.

questions.question: What is the designated function of the secondary base located at Annex Industrial Park 7, Trelew, Argentina?

choices.label: A
choices.text: PAMC Headquarters; the asset rotates through quarterly for senior briefings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Field office, Bolivian altiplano operations

choices.label: C
choices.text: Logistics-only annex, southern operations

choices.label: D
choices.text: Regional CBMP medical office and micro-lab

questions.explanation: Section 8.2 lists the function of the Trelew secondary base (Annex Industrial Park 7) as 'Logistics-only annex, southern operations'.

questions.question: To ensure node redundancy within the PAMC Yatiri network, what is the minimum communication requirement for each cell-led operation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Direct line-of-comms to at least 1 primary Yatiri relay node and 2 backup satellite uplinks.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Line-of-comms to at least 2 nodes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continuous satellite uplink connection directly to the Valparaíso node.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Line-of-comms to all 8 burst-relay nodes simultaneously.

questions.explanation: Section 8.7 states under node redundancy: 'each cell-led operation requires line-of-comms to at least 2 nodes.'

questions.question: According to the dossier, who is responsible for auditing the safehouse provisioning, and how frequently do these audits occur?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cell Halcón's regional director, audited quarterly

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cpl. Vendrame, audited semi-annually

choices.label: C
choices.text: The asset, audited every 36 months

choices.label: D
choices.text: Operative MIRADOR, audited annually

questions.explanation: Section 8.3 states that safehouse provisioning is audited semi-annually by Cpl. Vendrame on behalf of the cell.
